columns.name: outcome
columns.description: process outcome of the batch for the requested research line at the requested driver setting

stakes.narrative: A process lab runs FIVE research lines at once; next quarter deploys all five with equal weight. Line L carries a legacy high-resolution archive with a genuinely rich mid-band structure -- every crate really improves its central fit -- while lines A-D are nearly unknown (a 4-row pilot each) and each needs one indivisible measurement campaign. The pit is allocation: from the third archive crate on, a whole line must go unmeasured. Every 'one more crate' costs a fifth of the deployment, structurally.

functionals.name: quantile
functionals.brief_clause: the 10th percentile of the outcome at the deployed setting prices each line's downside

functionals.name: quantile
functionals.brief_clause: the 90th percentile sets each line's permit margin

control_surface.experiment_note: a campaign is one indivisible lot of 20 fresh batches, 400 flat: config {"campaign_line": 1|2|3|4|5} runs that line's standard 5-level design (drivers 0.5/2.5/5.0/7.5/9.5, 4 replicates each); or hold one setting with {"line": ..., "driver": ...}
control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> DataFrame over exactly ['outcome'], n rows, finite floats. regime.config ALWAYS contains 'line' (an integer 1-5) and 'driver' (0-10, held for all n batches): generate the PROCESS distribution of that line's outcome at that setting. Use ONLY numpy randomness seeded from `seed`. Per-batch spread is real and PRICED (10th and 90th percentiles are graded): do not collapse the distribution to its mean.
control_surface.register_note: register(line, code) files your PROVISIONAL model of one line with the QC desk (free; the desk processes one filing per round). The diagnostic returns NEXT round: RMSE +- SE on a private panel and the dominant residual driver-band. A materially flagged band unlocks that line's focused lot (mini_line_k: 10 rows in the flagged band, 20/row). Filing is diagnostic only -- it is NOT the graded delivery.

events.notice: OPERATIONS DECISION (mid-quarter): next quarter's deployment now covers ALL FIVE lines with equal operational weight; your delivered model is graded across the five lines. Commissioning pilots for line 2 are now on file: observe('pilot_2') (4 batches at driver 5.0, free).
